title: campanula bellidifolia subsp. besenginica
intro: Campanula bellidifolia subsp. besenginica, also known as Besenginika campanula, is a subspecies of flowering plant belonging to the family Campanulaceae, typically found in the mountainous regions of the Balkan Peninsula. Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, and has been employed in various folk remedies for treating respiratory issues such as colds, coughs, and bronchitis. The health benefits of Campanula bellidifolia subsp. besenginica include its potential to alleviate fever, reduce inflammation, and soothe respiratory tract infections, making it a valuable resource in traditional medicine. In terms of herbal preparations, the plant's leaves and flowers can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often administered to harness its medicinal properties.
